inputs.prompt: Video topic: How to negotiate your salary at a new job. Target audience: young professionals 22-35 starting their first or second job. Primary goal: tutorial. Channel tone: direct, no-fluff, practical.

Video topic: Understanding photogrammetry for archaeological site documentation. Target audience: archaeology students and early-career professionals. Primary goal: educational. Channel niche: digital archaeology tools.
Video topic: Cooking pasta. Target audience: beginners. Primary goal: tutorial.

Video topic: How the German Energiewende (energy transition) actually works in practice and where it has failed. Target audience: European policy watchers and climate-interested viewers. Primary goal: inform. Channel tone: journalistic, data-driven.
Video topic: Productivity tips. Target audience: everyone. Primary goal: help people.

Video topic: How Mexico City is sinking 50 centimetres per year due to groundwater extraction, and what the government is (and isn't) doing about it. Target audience: urban planning enthusiasts and Latin American viewers. Primary goal: inform. Channel tone: visual, dramatic but fact-based.

Video topic: Why your résumé gets rejected in 6 seconds. Working title (keep this): 'The 6-Second Rule: Why HR Throws Away Your CV'. Target audience: job seekers 20-35. Primary goal: tutorial. Channel tone: empathetic, practical.

Video topic: How Qatar built an entire national museum from scratch in under a decade, the controversy around imported cultural artefacts, and what it reveals about soft power strategy in the Gulf states. Target audience: architecture and geopolitics enthusiasts. Primary goal: analysis. Channel niche: cultural geopolitics.

Video topic: Morning routine for productivity. Target audience: entrepreneurs 25-40. Primary goal: tutorial. Channel tone: aspirational but grounded. Note: this keyword is extremely saturated — the title needs to stand out from thousands of similar videos.

Video topic: Why Japanese train punctuality is measured in seconds, not minutes, and the cultural and engineering systems that make it possible. Target audience: English-speaking viewers with an interest in Japan. Primary goal: cultural explainer. Channel tone: wonder, no exoticism. Consider that Japanese viewers may also watch — avoid clichés.
